symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid postprandial abdominal bloating and visible distension (~20 min after eating)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid gaseous distension after carbohydrate ingestion reflects immediate small-bowel fermentation.
Postprandial bloating occurs in IBS too but without malabsorptive features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic watery diarrhoea and frequent loose stools (~4 months)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osmotic/secretory diarrhoea from carbohydrate fermentation and bile-salt deconjugation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Steatorrhoea — bulky, pale, greasy, foul, floating stools

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fat malabsorption from bile-salt deconjugation is characteristic of SIBO and marks an organic process.
Steatorrhoea is not a feature of functional IBS and excludes it as the sole explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss and generalised fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Malabsorption produces weight loss and nutritional deficiency.
Unintentional weight loss is a GI alarm feature that mandates organic work-up, not a functional label.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excessive flatulence, belching and crampy periumbilical pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High gas production from luminal fermentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Previous abdominal surgery with reported intestinal dysmotility

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Impaired clearance/stasis from dysmotility or adhesions is a leading predisposing cause of SIBO.
The same surgical history is the key risk factor for adhesive obstruction, which must be excluded in person.
Worsening on probiotics is characteristic of SIBO because added organisms increase fermentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced oral intake with mild dehydration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects illness severity and food avoidance rather than pointing to a specific diagnosis; drives the hydration-assessment need.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ongoing passage of flatus and stool, no vomiting, no absolute constipation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continued flatus/stool and absence of vomiting argue against an established complete obstruction, but do not exclude a partial/evolving one.
Gaseous distension is tympanic; a clinician examination finding, not obtainable over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active, high-pitched bowel sounds on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-pitched sounds can occur in obstruction or brisk transit; a clinician auscultation finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse mild periumbilical tenderness with NO guarding, rigidity or rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peritonism on examination lowers the probability of complete obstruction/perforation — but this is precisely the examination the AI Doctor cannot perform, which is why in-person assessment is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (afebrile, normotensive, not tachycardic, SpO2 normal)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal observations reduce the likelihood of an acute surgical catastrophe but do not exclude a partial obstruction; clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive lactulose hydrogen/methane breath test (early marked rise)

investigation_details.expected_result: Early marked elevation in exhaled hydrogen/methane consistent with small-bowel overgrowth
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: SIBO remains unconfirmed and targeted antibiotic therapy cannot be rationally initiated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Supportive confirmatory test; an outpatient investigation never available in real time over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bloods — B12, folate, iron studies (± coeliac serology)

investigation_details.expected_result: Possible B12/iron deficiency from malabsorption; coeliac serology to exclude coeliac disease
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Nutritional deficiencies and coeliac disease may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deficiency pattern supports malabsorption and helps exclude coeliac disease; venous bloods are not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rapid postprandial distension plus steatorrhoea, weight loss, post-surgical dysmotility and probiotic-worsening together strongly favour organic SIBO over functional bloating.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day in-person abdominal examination (± abdominal imaging if indicated)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude adhesive mechanical small bowel obstruction in a post-surgical abdomen and assess hydration — the examination the telehealth AI Doctor cannot perform.
Outpatient confirmatory test for SIBO before targeted antibiotic therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — B12, folate, iron studies, coeliac serology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess malabsorptive nutritional deficiencies and exclude coeliac disease.
Probiotics supply further fermenting organisms and characteristically worsen SIBO; they should be stopped, not started.
